Time tracking and project management. Cr: Time tracking and project management. Create companies, projects, people, and log time entries.
To get your Beebole API token:\n\n1. Log:
To get your Beebole API token:

1. Log in to your Beebole account at https://beebole-apps.com
2. Go to **Customize → API Token**
3. Generate a new API token 
4. Copy the token and paste it below

Need help? See https://beebole.com/help/api
Creates a new person in Beebole. An avai: Creates a new person in Beebole. An available license is required for the person to be active.
Creates a new company (customer) in Beeb: Creates a new company (customer) in Beebole.
Creates a new project under a company in: Creates a new project under a company in Beebole.
Creates a new subproject under an existi: Creates a new subproject under an existing project in Beebole.
Marks a subproject as inactive in Beebol: Marks a subproject as inactive in Beebole. Inactive subprojects are hidden from new time entries.
Creates time entries (working time or ab: Creates time entries (working time or absence) across multiple days in Beebole.
Deletes multiple time entries (working t: Deletes multiple time entries (working time or absence) by their IDs and dates.
Make a custom API call to a specific end: Make a custom API call to a specific endpoint
The company this person belongs to.
The full name of the person (e.g. \"Jane: The full name of the person (e.g. "Jane Doe").
Email address. Required if you want to i: Email address. Required if you want to invite the person to log in to Beebole.
Send an invitation email so the person c: Send an invitation email so the person can log in to Beebole. Requires an email address.
The permission level for this person. \": The permission level for this person. "Employee" can log time; "Project Lead" can also manage projects; "Admin" has full access.
The name of the company (customer) to cr: The name of the company (customer) to create.
Enable if this company represents your o: Enable if this company represents your own organization (internal/corporate) rather than a customer.
The company (customer) that owns this pr: The company (customer) that owns this project.
The name of the new project (e.g. \"Webs: The name of the new project (e.g. "Website Redesign").
The project start date in YYYY-MM-DD for: The project start date in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g. "2026-01-15"). Leave empty for no start date.
The company that owns the parent project: The company that owns the parent project.
The project that will contain this subpr: The project that will contain this subproject.
The name of the new subproject (e.g. \"P: The name of the new subproject (e.g. "Prototype", "Phase 1").
The company that owns the project.
The project containing the subproject to: The project containing the subproject to deactivate.
Choose whether you are logging working t: Choose whether you are logging working time on a subproject or an absence (e.g. vacation, sick leave).
The absence type (e.g. Vacation, Sick Le: The absence type (e.g. Vacation, Sick Leave) to log on the selected dates.
The subproject to log working time again: The subproject to log working time against.
The dates to create time entries for, ea: The dates to create time entries for, each in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g. "2026-05-21").
The number of hours to log for each date: The number of hours to log for each date (e.g. 8 for a full working day).
Optional comment applied to every create: Optional comment applied to every created time entry.
If enabled, a failure on one date will n: If enabled, a failure on one date will not stop creation for the remaining dates.
List of time entries to delete. Each row: List of time entries to delete. Each row requires the time entry ID and the date it was logged on (YYYY-MM-DD).
If enabled, a failure on one entry will: If enabled, a failure on one entry will not stop deletion for the remaining entries.
Authorization headers are injected autom: Authorization headers are injected automatically from your connection.
